Kevin T. Barger of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Shelton was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Shelton's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The deposition testimony of George Kirk is irrelevant as plaintiffs are not claiming exposure to Kaiser Gypsum drywall. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' Exhibit A (Mr. Shelton's deposition testimony) are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Desiree Winn of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13645 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lindsay Weiss of MANION GAYNOR & MANNING (213-694-4206), appearing for defendant / moving party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Edward Shelton & Carol Shelton

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Shelton's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Shelton was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. As to the duty issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Shelton. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim that defendant owed Mr. Shelton a duty. As to the sophisticated user defense, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that such defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Plaintiffs' objections to Dr. Richard Cohen's declaration are overruled as there was no violation of CRC 3.1115 or C.C.P. section 422.30. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Desiree Winn of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13645 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
